description: Agents use facial recognition in real-time to obtain identities of people they encounter on patrol, and analysts use the technology to identify individuals from pictures or videos during subsequent investigations.

description: Bi2 provides a mobile app to ICE’s deportation arm that scans irises and links them to a database of previous iris scans for real-time identification.

description: These devices collect multiple biometric identifiers— such as fingerprint, iris, and face scans—that can then be fed into DHS databases for use during current or future investigations.

entity: Biometric collection from immigration benefit applicants
description: DHS collects fingerprints, facial and iris scans, and DNA from applicants for identification purposes. The agency is seeking to expand collection to application sponsors, including U.S. citizens.

description: The Automated Biometric Identification System (IDENT) is DHS’s legacy biometrics database. The agency has been working for years to transition to the Homeland Advanced Recognition System (HART), which will have expanded capacity for collecting and sharing biometric data. Many of DHS’s biometric technologies, such as Mobile Fortify, are connected to IDENT/HART.
